title: THE DEAD ENGINEER — ECHO DIRECTOR'S CUT V5
episodeObjective: Guide Hale from the relay room to personnel and medical records, prove why the station says he died, expose the 7.14 signal contradiction, and make an irreversible carrier decision that changes Episode 04.

designRules.appliedRuleSet: 30-point player experience, character, dialogue, humanization, consequence, pacing, and chain-effect framework.
designRules.choiceRule: Every visible choice group has exactly two options.
designRules.dialoguePolicy: No report-style action summaries. Characters experience actions with the operator instead of reporting completed actions.
designRules.coActionPolicy: Doors, ladders, panels, records, routes, and carrier controls pause for operator direction.
designRules.consequencePolicy: At least one irreversible consequence is present: cache evidence damage, unknown attention, or carrier amplification.
designRules.pacingPolicy: Shorter scenes are preferred when they preserve momentum and immersion.

removedPuzzles.hale_death_correlation_03: Removed. Replaced with operational two-choice inference so the player directs the theory instead of solving a matching puzzle.

e03_n01.speaker: HALE
e03_n01.objective: Get Hale out of the relay room and decide whether to chase 7.14 or verify his personnel file first.

e03_n02_714.speaker: HALE
e03_n02_714.objective: Hale explains 7.14 while the relay room becomes unstable.

events.text: Straight at the thing with teeth.

e03_n02_file_route.speaker: HALE
e03_n02_file_route.objective: Hale accepts the file-first plan and prepares for the ladder.

events.text: I hate how much I trust old bolts right now.
Don't ask if that was a scream.

e03_n04_force_door.speaker: HALE
e03_n04_force_door.objective: Force the personnel cache door, creating irreversible evidence damage.

e03_n04_panel.speaker: HALE
e03_n04_panel.objective: Open the personnel cache carefully and preserve evidence.

events.text: Someone clawed from the wrong side.

e03_n05_cache_inside_clean.speaker: HALE
e03_n05_cache_inside_clean.objective: Recover Hale's personnel file from intact cache.

e03_n05_cache_inside_damaged.speaker: HALE
e03_n05_cache_inside_damaged.objective: Recover damaged Hale file after forced entry.

e03_n05_adjacent_files.speaker: HALE
e03_n05_adjacent_files.objective: Check files near Hale before opening his own.

events.title: PERSONNEL FILE: ETHAN HALE
events.content:
DR. ETHAN HALE
ROLE: CHIEF ENGINEER
ASSIGNMENT: ORBITAL RESEARCH STATION ECHO
STATUS: DECEASED
TIME OF DEATH: 21:20
LOCATION: DOOR 12 ACCESS CORRIDOR
events.caption: Recovered personnel record from station cache.
events.message: [PERSONNEL FILE: ETHAN HALE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: So tell me what that means.
events.speaker: HALE

e03_n06_personnel_file_damaged.speaker: SYSTEM
e03_n06_personnel_file_damaged.objective: Reveal incomplete Hale death record because the cache was damaged.

events.title: DAMAGED PERSONNEL FILE: ETHAN HALE
events.content:
DR. ETHAN HALE
ROLE: CHIEF ENGINEER
STATUS: DECEASED
TIME OF DEATH: 21:20
LOCATION: [DATA LOST]
RECOVERY NOTE: CACHE DAMAGE CAUSED PERMANENT LOSS.
events.caption: Damaged personnel file recovered after forced cache entry.
events.message: [DAMAGED PERSONNEL FILE: ETHAN HALE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: I needed you to say that.

e03_n07_confront.speaker: HALE
e03_n07_confront.objective: Hale reacts defensively to the death record.

events.text: Please don't make your voice sound like the file.

e03_n07_reassure.speaker: HALE
e03_n07_reassure.objective: Hale steadies through the operator's reassurance.

events.text: I'm scared enough to be alive.
Lab smells like old chemicals from here.

e03_n09_glass_return.speaker: HALE
e03_n09_glass_return.objective: Reach medical archive after risky route.

events.text: I know how low that bar is.

e03_n09_lab_access.speaker: HALE
e03_n09_lab_access.objective: Use slower lab access and find a human memory.

events.text: Lin used tiny angry labels in here.

e03_n10_medical_archive.speaker: SYSTEM
e03_n10_medical_archive.objective: Recover autopsy fragment and choose signal or memory investigation.

events.title: AUTOPSY FRAGMENT: HALE
events.content:
SUBJECT: ETHAN HALE
CAUSE OF DEATH: DECOMPRESSION TRAUMA
LOCATION: DOOR 12 ACCESS CORRIDOR
RECOVERY STATUS: BODY NOT RECOVERED
NOTE: VITALS TERMINATED AT 21:20
ARCHIVE INTEGRITY: 68%
events.caption: Recovered emergency medical fragment.
events.message: [AUTOPSY FRAGMENT: HALE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Just a hole wearing my name.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I can't tell if it was fear.

e03_n11_signal_signature.speaker: HALE
e03_n11_signal_signature.objective: Analyze live signal and reveal 7.14 residue.

events.text: That made sense in my head.

e03_n12_timeline_conflict.speaker: SYSTEM
e03_n12_timeline_conflict.objective: Present the contradiction and force operational theory choice.

events.title: TIMELINE CONFLICT
events.content:
21:14 — Door 12 opened.
21:17 — Security override accepted.
21:20 — Ethan Hale declared deceased.
21:22 — Live communication signature still matches Ethan Hale.

Conflict: active communication signature exists after recorded time of death.
Secondary conflict: communication signature contains 7.14 MHz harmonic residue.
events.caption: Automated timeline conflict report.

events.text: Pick which liar we trust first.
events.speaker: HALE

e03_n13_record_wrong.speaker: HALE
e03_n13_record_wrong.objective: Hale takes comfort in trusting the signal.

e03_n13_signal_compromised.speaker: HALE
e03_n13_signal_compromised.objective: Hale is hurt by doubt but accepts the need for it.

e03_n14_other_voice.speaker: UNKNOWN
e03_n14_other_voice.objective: Reveal there is another voice and force a safety choice.

e03_n15_move_focus.speaker: HALE
e03_n15_move_focus.objective: Leave medical and reach carrier control without inviting the unknown.

events.text: Carrier control is two turns ahead.

e03_n15_listen_voice.speaker: UNKNOWN
e03_n15_listen_voice.objective: The operator listens and permanently attracts the unknown's attention.

events.text: I hate that I get why.
events.speaker: HALE

e03_n16_carrier_control.speaker: HALE
e03_n16_carrier_control.objective: Reach carrier control and decide whether to isolate or amplify 7.14.

events.text: I hate that amplify is even a word here.

e03_n17_isolate.speaker: HALE
e03_n17_isolate.objective: Isolate 7.14 and end with a restrained clue.

events.text: whose voice are you hearing now?
events.speaker: HALE

e03_n17_amplify.speaker: HALE
e03_n17_amplify.objective: Amplify 7.14 and create irreversible Episode 04 contamination.

events.text: Whatever comes next belongs to both of us.
events.speaker: HALE

episode04UsageNotes.irreversibleCacheDamage: Some record data is permanently lost; Hale can reference the operator's responsibility.
episode04UsageNotes.listenedToUnknown: UNKNOWN becomes more direct and personal in Episode 04.
episode04UsageNotes.isolated714: Episode 04 starts quieter but with unresolved dual-signal tension.
episode04UsageNotes.amplified714: Episode 04 starts with stronger contamination and Hale blames both himself and the operator.
episode04UsageNotes.episode04Theory: Hale's trust tone changes depending on whether the player trusted the signal or doubted it.

directorCut.sourceStrategy: Rebuilt Episode_03 from the uploaded Lifeline Cut V6 into a tighter, more consequence-driven, co-action episode.
majorChanges: Removed long report-style monologues and replaced them with shorter in-the-moment dialogue.
Added co-action decisions around ladder, personnel cache, route choice, medical archive, timeline theory, unknown voice, and carrier control.
Added real irreversible consequences: cache damage, unknown attention, and carrier amplification.
Reduced system narration and removed matching puzzle repetition.
Made trust and pressure visible through Hale's dialogue rather than only effects.
Strengthened Episode 04 setup with clear start flags.
